title: carex rufa subsp. moenchiana
intro: Carex rufa subsp. moenchiana, commonly known as red sedge, is a type of grass-like perennial plant belonging to the family Cyperaceae. Traditionally, red sedge has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, red sedge is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and it can also be used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
